Override.tagger.title.unrecognized: Unrecognized Steam Tag. \nTag not saved on Custom List, Right-click to save it.
Override.tagger.label.name: TAG NAME
Override.tagger.tooltip.addnew: Click to create a new custom user tag.
Override.tagger.error.invalidtagname: Invalid tag name. Only letters, numbers, spaces, and underscores are allowed.
Override.tagger.tag.submarine.desc: The primary player-controllable vessels, containing hulls, gaps, structures, items, and waypoints for navigation.
Override.tagger.tag.mission.desc: Defines round objectives such as salvage, assassination, or monster hunts, interacting with specific location types.
Override.tagger.tag.item.desc: Fundamental building blocks of modding, defined by unique identifiers and components like weapons, machines, or containers.
Override.tagger.tag.itemassembly.desc: Serialized groups of existing items and wiring that serve as prefabs for complex logic or modular room layouts.
Override.tagger.tag.monster.desc: Character configuration files defining species' ragdoll physics, animations, and artificial intelligence behaviors.
Override.tagger.tag.language.desc: Localization packages that override game text or add support for new languages using the infotexts root element.
Override.tagger.tag.eventset.desc: Collections of random occurrences, such as monster spawns or environmental hazards, governed by the game's event manager.
Override.tagger.tag.totalconversion.desc: Aggressive modifications that replace the core game package to redefine jobs, map generation, and fundamental systems.
Override.tagger.tag.art.desc: Visual overrides for game assets, including new textures for structures, sprites, and graphical enhancements.
Override.tagger.tag.environment.desc: Procedural level objects including undersea plants, rock formations, and vents used in environmental generation.
Override.tagger.tag.outpost.desc: Static modules stitched into levels at specific points, serving as civilian, military, or research hubs.
Override.tagger.tag.beaconstation.desc: Secondary objectives requiring specific internal logic, such as sonar and power supply, to be considered repaired.
Override.tagger.tag.wreck.desc: Salvageable debris and sunken ships that often host unique biological hazards like Thalamus infestations.
Override.tagger.tag.ruin.desc: Ancient alien environments consisting of distinct structures, traps, and entities like Fractal Guardians.
Override.tagger.tag.weapons.desc: Sub-category of items used for identifying offensive tools and weapons for filtering and merchant logic.
Override.tagger.tag.equipment.desc: Sub-category of items representing wearable tools, gear, and suits used by characters for various tasks.
Override.tagger.tag.medical.desc: Sub-category of items including medicines, chemicals, and treatment tools for healing and afflictions.
Override.tagger.tag.gameplaymechanics.desc: Modifications that alter the simulation state, including physics, health afflictions, and character skill systems.
Override.tagger.tag.qol.desc: Quality of Life improvements that focus on simplifying interactions or providing information without changing core rules.
Override.tagger.tag.client-side.desc: Local modifications installed only on the player's machine, typically including visual changes or UI enhancements.
Override.tagger.tag.server-side.desc: Logic-altering modifications that affect server behavior and typically require clients to have matching content.
Override.tagger.tag.outdated.desc: A maintenance indicator signaling that a mod may cause crashes or synchronization errors with the current game version.
Override.tagger.tag.gamemode.desc: Modifications that change the fundamental structure of a round, such as new play styles or PvP scenarios.
Override.tagger.tag.library.desc: Foundational resources and frameworks, such as scripting modules or utility libraries, used by other mods.
Override.tagger.tag.moddertool.desc: Specialized tools and utilities designed to assist modders in creating and testing content.
Override.tagger.tag.misc.desc: Miscellaneous content that does not fit into other specific Workshop categories.
